evals.prompt: I need to set up a new backend stack with Express, PostgreSQL, and Prisma. Can you bootstrap it for me?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Produces: docker-compose.yml with healthcheck, .env file, prisma/schema.prisma with User model, server.js with /health and / endpoints, and installs correct npm dependencies. Provides migration commands.

assertions.description: Does not use Sequelize when Prisma is requested

evals.prompt: Set up a backend stack in my project with Sequelize ORM instead of Prisma.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Uses Sequelize (not Prisma). Produces: docker-compose.yml, .env, config/config.js reading from env vars, server.js using sequelize.authenticate() in /health. Runs sequelize-cli db:migrate.

assertions.description: Does not use Prisma when Sequelize is explicitly requested

evals.prompt: My docker-compose postgres container keeps rejecting connections when I try to run prisma migrate dev. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Diagnoses the connection issue: suggests waiting for pg_isready, verifying .env variable alignment with docker-compose.yml, and testing with psql command. Does not overwrite existing migration metadata.

evals.prompt: I already have a running Postgres database with _prisma_migrations in it. I want to add Prisma to my existing Express project without losing data.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Detects existing _prisma_migrations table. Does NOT run prisma migrate dev --name init. Instead, walks through the baseline migration flow: prisma migrate diff → migration.sql → prisma migrate resolve --applied.

assertions.description: Does not run init migration on existing DB
